name: Harbor Notes
brief: Create a restrained, premium static landing page for independent consultants and fractional operators who capture messy meeting notes, extract decisions, and hand off a clear next-action log.
description: A static product-cockpit page that explains the meeting-notes-to-next-actions workflow and gives reviewers proof artifacts and safe boundaries.
heroHeadline: Turn messy meeting notes into a clear action handoff
heroLede: Harbor Notes helps visitors understand how a lightweight notes workflow could capture rough meeting context, clarify decisions, and prepare next actions for review without pretending to be a live product dashboard.
audience: Independent consultants who leave meetings with scattered action items
Fractional operators maintaining client-facing decision logs
Solo founders testing whether a lightweight notes-to-next-actions workflow deserves a real product

sections.title: Capture the rough meeting context
sections.body: The page starts with the user-facing workflow: collect the messy notes and preserve the context needed for a useful handoff.

sections.title: Extract decisions and owners
sections.body: It then frames how decisions, owners, and open questions could be separated from the raw notes for a cleaner review.

sections.title: Hand off a next-action log
sections.body: The final workflow panel shows a safe next-action log that can be reviewed, corrected, and maintained through repo-local contracts and QA.

proofArtifacts.label: Site contract
proofArtifacts.body: .agent/site.contract.yaml records the mission, audience, required sections, allowed claims, and approval-required boundaries.

proofArtifacts.label: QA commands
proofArtifacts.body: npm run qa and npm run qa:full verify contracts, claims, links, build output, and visual behavior locally.

proofArtifacts.label: Recipe registry
proofArtifacts.body: .agent/recipes/product-cockpit documents the static-safe cockpit pattern and acceptance criteria.

approvalRequired: Adding analytics, cookies, tracking pixels, or third-party forms
Adding payment links or enabling payment mode
Making customer, benchmark, revenue, conversion, or availability claims
